title: Laryngitis
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Laryngitis is swelling and irritation of the voice box that can cause hoarseness or voice loss.
Common causes include viral illness, voice overuse, and irritation.
Voice rest, fluids, humidified air, and avoiding smoke or irritants can support recovery.
Breathing trouble, drooling, severe throat pain, neck swelling, or prolonged symptoms require separate clinician-directed care.
